text: So, a few days ago, this post went super
viral of someone who told their Denny's
waiter to round up to $70 for the tip.
And then the waiter used their employee
discount on the order so that they could
make their tip bigger. Honestly, smart
of him because it doesn't affect the
customer. But that's when Denny's had
the audacity to reply to this tweet.
They said, "Thank you for making us
aware." And then asked for all of the
order information as if the person who
they did not. I don't know why Denny's
thought this person would side with a
corporation and get an employee fired
over 13 bucks, but luckily that did not
